steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The underlined sentence claims that the depiction of Esperanza encouraged Latina authors specifically to use fiction to examine experiences from their own youth. Supporting evidence must directly link the book to Latina authors writing about their own adolescence.

steps.title: Eliminate weak evidence and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice:
  • Choice A — In interviews, a number of Latina authors say that The House on Mango Street inspired them to write about their own adolescence in their novels.: Latina authors directly testifying to this inspiration is exactly the evidence the claim needs. ✓
  • Choice B — In published writings, several prominent authors who are not Latina say that reading The House on Mango Street influenced their approach to writing fiction.: non-Latina authors — wrong group; does not support the claim about Latina authors. ✗
  • Choice C — The House on Mango Street has sold over six million copies and is one of the most commonly read books among high school and university students in the US.: popularity data says nothing about how Latina authors were inspired. ✗
  • Choice D — Since 1984, new novels about young Latina characters by Latina authors have often been compared to The House on Mango Street.: similarity does not prove inspiration; the comparison could arise from shared cultural context alone. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
